title: Bug: field locking did not cascade to related (event-connected) fields
description: get-related-fn walked Domino's events graph with field IDs, but the graph is keyed by model PATHS — so it always returned only #{id} and locking a field locked nothing else. Editing :kg left :m and :bmi (which it feeds) editable by other users, racing the computation. Fix: map id->path, walk the path-keyed graph (input+output), map reachable paths back to lock-ids (keyword for top-level, the path vector for collection-item fields so the cascade stays per-item).
close_reason: get-related-fn id->path mapping fixed; co-locks event-connected fields; 221 tests + storyboard green

title: Phase 7 (§15.1-2): versioned form store + pinned document record
description: Immutable form version archive with content digest; documents pin exact (form-id, version, digest) at creation and load THAT version (not latest); auto-migration becomes opt-in rebase; richer document record (:status, :meta, :rev). See PLAN.md §15.1-15.2, §15.15 step 1.
close_reason: Immutable version archive + pin-at-create + load-pinned + opt-in rebase + richer doc record. 105 tests + storyboard green.

title: Authentication & real user identity
description:
Replace the per-page-load 'uid' signal token with real authenticated users. uid currently only correlates SSE connection + POSTs for locking/presence; it is not a security boundary. Needs: login/session, user records, authz on document access/edit, and wiring user identity through locks/presence instead of the uid token.

## Context
Phase 6. See PLAN.md. Locking/presence currently keyed by a client-generated uid signal.
close_reason: Closed

title: Make apply-lock! owner-aware (re-locking own/co-locked field is not a conflict)
description: With the lock cascade, focusing a field co-locked into the user's own set (or re-focusing before blur releases) returned :conflict because conflicting-locks counted the user's own held lock. Fix: in apply-lock!, ignore conflicts owned by the locking user (remove (partial owns-lock? user) ...). A different user's lock still conflicts. Covered by locks-test.
close_reason: apply-lock! ignores same-user locks; 222 tests green

title: Refactor: extract form compilation from forms.clj -> forms_compile.clj
description: Deferred Phase 2 item. forms.clj is the form store but requires cascades/imports/validation/partials only for prepare-form (partials splice + validation/cascade compile + import-effects). Move prepare-form, compile-import-effects, and the prepared accessors get-form/get-form-version to forms_compile.clj (requires forms + cascades/imports/validation/partials). forms.clj becomes a pure store returning raw EDN, no upward deps. Repoint ~17 call sites.
close_reason: forms_compile.clj extracted; forms.clj now pure store; 217 tests green

title: Refactor: extract landing/index views from cells/document.clj -> web/landing.clj
description: Deferred Phase 2 item. cells/document.clj (455 LOC) mixes the landing+index-lookup hiccup views (create-control/doc-row/landing-styles/landing-html/index-page-html, lines 41-121) with the document-lifecycle cells. Move the views to web/landing.clj (flat web/ convention, alongside layout.clj) so the cell is parse->orchestrate->view like cells/form.clj. Views used only within this file.
close_reason: web/landing.clj extracted; cell now parse->orchestrate->view; 217 tests + storyboard green

title: Refactor: extract table view-state from session.clj -> view_state.clj
description: Deferred Phase 2 item. Sort/filter/page/row-order/column-overlay funcs (presentation, not document data) live in session.clj mixed with the live-editing primitives. Move set-table-* / move-item! / update-view! to view_state.clj. Coupling: item-keys is shared with clear-items! (collection op, stays) — make it a public session primitive. Callers: cells/form.clj + collections_test only.
close_reason: view_state.clj extracted; item-keys kept as session primitive; 217 tests + storyboard green

title: Refactor: extract signals.clj + endpoints.clj from render.clj
description: Deferred Phase 2 item. render.clj (411 LOC) overloads the Datastar wire vocabulary (signal-name/$/item-signal-name/signal maps) and the URL scheme (field-post-url/lock/unlock/coll-item-url) with the markup walk. Extract to signals.clj and endpoints.clj so the renderer doesn't own routes and the wire vocab is its own module. ~30 caller files import render just for these; repoint them. No behaviour change.
close_reason: signals.clj + endpoints.clj extracted; 217 tests + 32 storyboard workflows green

title: Architecture refactor: structure for maintainability + LLM-friendliness
description: Phased structural refactor on branch arch-refactor (see REFACTOR_PLAN.md). Dead-code removal, de-duplication (single sources of truth), split overloaded modules (render/session/cells.document/forms/table), naming disambiguation, and documentation/signposts. Pure structure — no behaviour changes; every phase test+storyboard verified.
close_reason: All phases complete (REFACTOR_PLAN.md): module splits (signals/endpoints, view-state, landing, forms-compile, table.js), dedups (edn-dir, web/request, option readers, append-meta!, components.bind), Phase 5 store helpers, naming + docs. Merged to main. 222 tests + full storyboard green.

title: Multi-step action resilience: idempotency, retry & compensation
description: Phase 10 deferred. A workflow action's side-effect steps (email/pdf/http) run in directives/apply! as a bare loop calling effects/perform! with no isolation: a failing step aborts the rest, the state transition (set-state, run FIRST) commits even on partial failure, retries re-run completed steps, and nothing compensates. Make action steps a saga: run side-effects idempotently (skip steps already logged :ok in [:meta :effects]) with per-step retry, commit the transition ONLY after all effects succeed, and on failure run declared :compensate intents for completed steps (reverse order) + record the partial. Engine-emitted (data-change) effects keep their fire-and-forget perform-all!.

title: Drafts authoring flow for the form editor
description: Phase 8 deferred. The live editor saves straight to the working form + publishes a version on every save, so authors can't iterate without affecting the live form new documents pin to. Add a draft slot per form: the editor Save writes a DRAFT (no publish, working form + new-doc pinning unchanged); a Publish action promotes the draft to the working form + a new published version and clears the draft; Discard drops the draft. The editor loads the draft if one exists; preview shows the in-editor content. New documents keep using the last published version until publish.
close_reason: Drafts authoring flow: per-form draft slot (both store backends), editor Save->draft / Publish->promote+version / Discard, edit-page opens the draft. New documents stay on the last published version until publish. Contract tests both backends + storyboard scenario 32.

title: Optimistic concurrency: reject stale document writes via :rev
description: Phase 8/11 deferred. :rev is bumped on every document write but never checked. Add a stale-write guard for the consequential, decision-committing actions (submit, revise, workflow transitions, delete): the rendered doc seeds a $rev signal that tracks the latest rev the client has received over SSE; these POSTs carry it; the server applies the action only if the posted rev matches the document's current rev, else rejects with 409 + a live notice prompting a refresh. Field edits stay live (authoritative session + field locking already handle them). Foundation for multi-node; defensive on single-node when SSE is lagging/disconnected.
close_reason: Optimistic concurrency via data-revision :rev: seed + SSE-broadcast $rev, consequential actions (workflow/submit/revise) reject stale rev with 409 + notice. :rev tracks DATA only (lifecycle/meta touch modified-at) so a client's own action never self-stales. Unit + FSM + storyboard (scenario 31) coverage.

title: Adopt Domino's transact + effects (incremental engine) in the editor seam
description:
Today editor/data.cljc uses Domino as a pure recompute function: build-ctx passes only :model+:events to d/initialize, and transact-ctx does apply-ops + full d/initialize REBUILD on every change. This discards (a) Domino effects (never run), (b) change-triggered event semantics (events re-fire for all PRESENT inputs each rebuild -> a constant clearing event pins its child), and (c) the transaction change-set. The rebuild was a collections workaround (no subcontexts).

Goal: use d/transact for value edits (persistent ctx; rebuild only on structural collection add/remove), re-derive eager reactions after each transact, and route change-triggered side-effects through Domino :effects.

Unlocks: cascade-clears as effects (fires on change, full-depth, no pinning, one txn); imports (fetch-on-change) as effects instead of imports.clj/run-imports!; precise re-render driven by the transaction :changes set instead of graph-walking. Unifies state+effects in one DAG.

Risk: data.cljc is the seam everything sits on (collections, reactions, locks, cascades, imports). Scope as a spike first; heavy regression tests. Current cascade impl (stepvine-9wo) is correct/tested — this is the right-way refactor, not a bugfix.
close_reason: Phase 1: transact-ctx uses domino.core/transact for value edits (change-triggered events + effects + change report), rebuild only on structural collection changes (coll-signature). Phase 2: cascade clearing moved back onto the Domino event DAG via synthesized clearing events (no pinning under transact); re-render only follows the UI graph. 164 tests + storyboard green. Imports-as-effects + change-set-driven render remain as future enhancements.

title: Parity: Granular per-view / per-field permissions
description: Ibis :roles grant per-view and per-field permissions (read vs write vs submit, e.g. {:mrn "read" :default "submit"}). Stepvine RBAC is per-FORM access (role intersection) only. Add granular permissions: per-view and per-field read/write/submit, enforced server-side.
close_reason: Granular per-field permissions: :write-roles (read-only render + 403 enforcement) and :read-roles (hidden). render/perm-ok? + render-node, render-doc-view seeds perm ctx, apply-field enforces via access/role-permitted?, forms/review.edn demo. permissions_test + storyboard scenario 21 + curl-verified 403. 176 tests pass.

title: Parity: Creation-time field hydration (session, today, defaults)
description: Ibis hydrations auto-populate fields on document create from the session identity (created-by), today's date, and literals. Stepvine has :imports (client/http/static) and export-time :fn/uuid/:fn/date, but cannot inject created-by / current date / literal defaults into fields at creation. Add session/today/literal hydration sources for the :create trigger.
close_reason: Creation-time hydration: docs/hydrate! resolves a form :hydrate map ({:session path}/{:today}/{:now}/{:value}) at creation; wired into do-create (plain + index paths); forms/note.edn demo. hydration_test + storyboard scenario 20 green. 174 tests pass.

title: Parity: Numeric / value display formatting (:fmt)
description: Ibis formats displayed values with printf-style :fmt (e.g. :fmt "%.2f" on :labeled-value/:numeric). Stepvine's :fmt only formats dropdown OPTION labels; there is no display formatting for value/labeled-value/number widgets, so computed money/measurements show raw float noise. Add :fmt-style display formatting to the value/number display widgets.
close_reason: Added printf-style :fmt to value/labeled-value/label widgets: render/fmt-value (server) + fmt-text-expr (live datastar data-text), handling %.Nf/%d/prefix/suffix/coercion/edge cases. order.edn money fields now currency-formatted. value_format_test + storyboard scenario 16 green. 159 tests pass.

title: Parity: Cascading / dependent dropdowns
description: Ibis dropdowns support :filters so one select's options depend on another field's value (e.g. city filtered by province). Stepvine resolves field options once at render (options/resolve-field-options) with no dependency on other field values. Add dependent option resolution that re-queries when an upstream field changes.
close_reason: Cascading dropdowns: :depends-on + per-option :when filtering in the dropdown widget, render/dependent-dropdown-nodes finder, apply-field rerender-dependents! (re-render + clear + SSE element patch on parent change), forms/booking.edn demo (region→clinic). cascading_dropdown_test + storyboard scenario 18 green. 161 tests pass.

title: Parity: Server-side async typeahead / search widget
description: Ibis :serverside-typeahead queries a datasource as you type (remote or large local sets). Stepvine's :typeahead is an HTML5 datalist that filters a fixed, pre-rendered option list client-side. Add a server-backed typeahead that fetches matches over SSE/fetch from an allowlisted source as the user types.
close_reason: Server-side typeahead: search-select widget (query signal -> debounced POST -> morph matches; pick sets field via field endpoint), web/search.clj handler (resolve form source, filter server-side via the query arity, one-shot element patch), lookup.edn demo. search_test + storyboard scenario 19 green. 172 tests pass.

title: Parity: Document assignment and routing
description: Aviary routes documents to roles via state + an assignee field; stepvine has role-gated actions but no per-document assignment. Add an assignee (user and/or role) on a document, the ability for a workflow step to (re)assign it, and per-assignee/per-role queues (pairs with the work-queue task).
close_reason: Document assignment: documents/assign! + assignee meta + history; workflow :assign step (templated :to) for auto-routing on transition; assignee doc-access; queue Assignee column + Claim button (assign self). assignment_test + storyboard scenario 17 (claim) green. 156 tests pass.

title: Parity: Work-queue / task-list views (documents by workflow state)
description: Aviary exposes work queues: document-search pages filtered by state-id (and an assignee/physician field) so each role sees its pending tasks. Stepvine's landing only lists a user's own documents per form; the doc store CAN filter by status (documents.clj:79) but there is no cross-user, state-filtered queue UI. Add work-queue views listing documents by workflow state (and assignee/role).
close_reason: Work queues: access/team-member? (role-restricted forms have a team of role holders + admins; open forms stay private), team-aware wrap-doc-access, web/queue.clj (/queue index + /queue/:form grouped by workflow state across owners), navbar link. access+security unit tests + storyboard scenario 17 green. 153 tests pass.

title: Parity: Email notification workflow step (SMTP)
description: Aviary has an :email workflow action that sends templated HTML/text mail with recipients/subject/body computed from document data and environment. Stepvine workflow steps are in-app only (:notify broadcasts over SSE to connected users); there is no outbound email. Add an :email step type (SMTP, e.g. postal) with templated subject/body and recipient resolution from fields/reactions.
close_reason: Implemented :email workflow step: pluggable mailer (recording dev/test + SMTP prod via postal), run-step :email with vector-concat templating ({:from}/{:reaction}), directive + resources wiring, admin Outbox page. workflow_email_test + storyboard scenario 10 (outbox) green. 151 tests pass.

title: Feature parity with ibis (forms/widgets) + aviary (workflows)
description: Umbrella for closing functionality gaps found by comparing stepvine against the reference systems ~/src/uhn/ibis (form/widget catalog) and ~/src/uhn/aviary (clinical workflow engine). Different definition format; this tracks FUNCTIONALITY parity (widget types, field features, workflow actions, routing/queues, RBAC granularity). Child tasks are prefixed 'Parity:'.
close_reason: All parity child tasks complete (21 closed): widget catalog (search-select, tree-select, grid, section-nav, advanced date-picker, modal data-entry, HTML/markdown reports, coded value-set fields), field features (granular permissions, cross-field validation, hydration), workflow actions (email/http/notify/pdf/snapshot via unified effects performer), routing/queues, RBAC granularity, server-side typeahead, cascading dropdowns via Domino DAG. Stepvine now at functional parity with ibis/aviary references.

title: Editor/admin navbar showed 'Sign in' while logged in; preview showed read-only banner
description: Two bugs on admin/editor pages: (1) auth/current-user is [users-store request] but admin.clj + editor.clj called it (req users-store) — reversed args read user-id off the store, returning nil, so the navbar rendered 'Sign in' for a logged-in admin. (2) The live preview is static HTML (no datastar), so the data-show lifecycle chrome (.sv-notice red bar, .sv-status submitted/read-only banner) rendered visible. Fixed arg order at all 4 call sites; preview hides .sv-notice/.sv-status.
close_reason: Fixed: reversed auth/current-user args at 4 call sites (navbar now shows the signed-in user on admin/editor pages); preview hides .sv-notice/.sv-status lifecycle chrome. Storyboard scenario 14 asserts both. 148 tests + storyboard green.

title: In-place page switching (datastar morph) + validation gating + per-page URLs
description: Multi-page forms now switch pages without a full reload: a datastar element-morph response (POST /doc/:id/page/:vid) replaces the view region + tabs + prev/next on the requesting client only (per-user nav, not broadcast). Each page keeps a real ?view= URL (linkable + deep-linkable + back/forward); the address bar updates via pushState. Forward progress is gated on a page's :valid reaction (Next aria-disabled + inert + hint until valid).
close_reason: Done: in-place page switch via datastar element-morph (POST /doc/:id/page/:vid, per-client SSE one-shot, no reload); per-page ?view= URLs (pushState + linkable + deep-link + popstate reload on back/forward); forward gating on a page's :valid reaction (Next aria-disabled + guarded + hint, live). Storyboard scenario 15 + 148 tests green.

title: Navbar UX + multi-page forms (views as pages with breadcrumbs/prev-next)
description: Navbar: user avatar, clear name+role, obvious Sign out, Sign in when logged out, Apps link to the editor. Multi-page forms: a form's :pages list turns its views into ordered pages over one shared data model; platform renders breadcrumb tabs (top) + prev/next (bottom); navigation is ?view= so session data persists across pages. Fixed resolve-field-options to pass through literal inline option vectors. Demo: onboarding.edn (Account/Profile/Review).
close_reason: Navbar: avatar + name + role badge, obvious Sign out, Sign in when logged out, Apps/Admin links (Apps = the editor entry from the main screen). Multi-page forms: :pages turns views into ordered pages over one shared model with breadcrumb tabs + prev/next; ?view nav preserves session data across pages; onboarding.edn demo. Fixed resolve-field-options literal-vector crash. 148 tests + storyboard scenario 15 green.

title: Live app editor (admin): CodeMirror EDN+CSS + live preview
description: Admin split-panel app editor: CodeMirror (CM6 + clojure-mode, ported from sporulator-ui) for the app EDN + CSS, with a live static preview iframe. Create-new-app scaffold, associate with roles, save live to the DB-backed app store (no redeploy).
close_reason: Admin split-panel app editor: CodeMirror (CM6 + clojure-mode/css from CDN, ported from sporulator-ui) EDN+CSS panes + live static preview iframe; create-new-app scaffold; role association; save live to the DB-backed app store. Plus basic user management. 148 tests + storyboard scenario 14 green.

title: DB-backed app/form store: FormStore protocol + SQLite backend
description: Make :store/forms pluggable behind a FormStore protocol; add an embedded SQLite backend (forms working table with EDN+CSS, form_versions sealed archive with version+digest) seeded from disk; keep the map/atom backend for tests. Postgres-ready (plain SQL over next.jdbc). Apps now live in the DB, editable live.
close_reason: FormStore protocol + MapFormStore (default/tests) + SqlFormStore (SQLite: forms working table + form_versions sealed archive, seeded from disk, Postgres-ready). dev/prod on SQLite; 148 tests (both-backend contract) + storyboard green; 8 apps live in data/apps.db.

title: App-owned styling: per-app CSS served live
description: Each app (form EDN) carries its own CSS via a sibling <id>.css loaded into the form record's :css, served live at /app/:id/style.css and layered over the platform widget CSS. CSS is live (not version-pinned) so re-skins apply without redeploy. First step toward apps-as-EDN+CSS-in-the-DB units.
close_reason: Per-app CSS: sibling <id>.css loaded into form :css, served live at /app/:id/style.css, layered over platform widget CSS, not version-pinned (live re-skin). 145 tests + storyboard (button re-skinned) green.

title: Role-based form access + admin UI
description: Users carry :roles; forms associate with roles via :store/access. Access-filtered landing grouped by form; admin UI (/admin/users, /admin/forms) to assign user roles and form roles; :admin role gates the admin UI and sees all forms. Access-checked document creation; role-aware workflow guards.
close_reason: Roles model + access store + access-filtered landing + admin UI (users/forms roles) + access-checked creation + role-aware workflow guards. 143 tests + storyboard scenario 13 green.

title: Phase 10 (§15.10-12): workflow state machine + step dispatcher
description: Data-defined validated state machine; pluggable run-step multimethod; in-process directive layer; idempotency+compensation; uniform external-client protocol. PLAN.md §15.10-15.12.
close_reason: Document state machine expressed as a mycelium FSM (states=cells, transitions=:edges, guard=:dispatches, :default→reject); validated :workflow graph; pluggable run-step multimethod; in-process directive layer. 127 tests + storyboard scenario 10 (open→review→closed) green. External-client protocol + multi-step compensation deferred until real external steps land.

title: Phase 8 (§15.3-5): lifecycle, durable audit, submission/approval
description: Audit store (before/after diffs, per-doc index, transitions); durability hardening (:rev stale-base rejection, never System/exit, lock/presence store abstractions); per-view submission + approvals + post-submit read-only. PLAN.md §15.3-15.5.
close_reason: Durable audit (diffs, per-doc, never-crash) + per-view submission/approval/snapshot + sole-editor/validity guards + hard read-only finalization. 111 tests + storyboard scenario 9 green. Multi-node rev-rejection + shared lock store deferred to Phase 11.

title: Phase 9 (§15.6-9): unified sources, imports, validation, partials
description: One :kind multimethod source resolver with host allowlist; lazy diff-based chainable imports; validation vocabulary compiled to reactions; load-time reusable partials. PLAN.md §15.6-15.9.
close_reason: Unified :kind source resolver + host allowlist; lazy/diff/chainable imports; validation vocabulary→reactions+:valid?; load-time partials. 119 tests + storyboard green. Forms served through splice→compile, all sci-sandboxed.

title: Calendar widget: persist edits to the document like other widgets
description: The calendar (CalendarJS) is currently display/read-only — its onupdate write-back was dropped when removing the broken DS global. Wire onupdate to POST edited events to the field endpoint so schedule edits persist in the document and broadcast to other viewers, like every other widget.
close_reason: Calendar onupdate POSTs to the field endpoint (datastar-request header, {<id>_events: json} body); verified end-to-end over HTTP (204 + persists across reload).

title: re-com feature parity: port remaining UI components
description: Port re-com UI elements for form-building parity. First batch (this issue): tabs, progress-bar, throbber, hyperlink, input-password, title, paragraph, h-box/v-box. Remaining backlog (follow-up issues): multi-select (dual list), tree-select, selection-list, popover, modal-panel, alert-list, info-button, scroller/border layout, nested-grid. All components emit only HTML structure styled by the external theme.
close_reason: Batch 1 ported: tabs, progress-bar, throbber, hyperlink, input-password, title, paragraph, h-box/v-box (commit 99a21c0). Remaining components filed as follow-ups.

title: External CSS theming: extract widget CSS to swappable stylesheet
description: Components should emit only HTML structure; styling comes from an external stylesheet served from resources/public/css and linkable per-form via a theme opt. Move the inline <style> out of form.html into a default theme stylesheet, remove inline :style attrs from widgets, and add a per-form theme override mechanism.
close_reason: Closed

title: Make table widget functional + nested collections
description: Implement table view-state (sort/page/filter/row-order/columns) stored per-document in the session; apply in table widget render; wire coll-sort/page/filter/move-row/clear cells + fix rerender to find table nodes. Support nested collections (collection within a collection item) via live-schema recursion on domino's nested model+events. Add a comprehensive demo form exercising all widgets.
close_reason: Table core ops functional (sort/page/row-reorder/clear/add/remove) via per-document view-state; comprehensive widget-showcase demo form added; nested collections supported in the engine (recursive expand-item on domino nested model+events) + tested. All on domino 0.4.0.

title: Port vendored editor to current domino API (master/0.4.0)
description: The vendored yogthos.stepvine.editor.{data,impl,locks} + render.clj target the legacy monolithic domino 0.4.0-alpha.3 API (~16 fns: get-downstream/get-upstream/get-parents/select/set-value/remove-value/update-child/remove-child/transaction-report/db/path/id-/not-lockable/transact/initialize + ::d/ internal ctx keywords subcontexts/collection?/elements/id->opts). domino master/0.4.0 is a ground-up rewrite (core exposes only transact/initialize/initial-transaction/trigger-effects; internals split into events/graph/model/rx/validation with a different ctx shape). Port the editor layer + render collections-data to the new API so we track the maintained library.
close_reason: Ported editor.data + render to domino 0.4.0: reactions computed eagerly by the editor (dependency-ordered), collections projected onto nested paths with synthetic flat ids + live-schema rebuilt per transact, lock relatedness recomputed from the events graph with per-item lock parents. 68 tests + e2e storyboard green; random-uuid warnings gone.

title: Rename procyon references to stepvine
description: Vendor procyon.editor.* into yogthos.stepvine.editor.*, update all requires, rename widget vocabulary procyon.components->stepvine.components and procyon.util->stepvine.util across forms/*.edn + render.clj + tests, drop procyon-editor/src from deps.edn :paths.
close_reason: vendored 6 editor files into src/clj/yogthos/stepvine/editor*, rewrote requires, widget vocab (stepvine.components/util), prose mentions, dropped procyon path from deps.edn. Tests + storyboard green.

title: Collection-item dropdown-select is not item-scoped
description: The dropdown-select widget renders data-bind="ftype" (top-level signal) instead of item-scoped fields_<idx>_ftype, posts to /field/:fid instead of the collection item endpoint, has no focus/blur lock, and renders no options for item fields. Builder field-type picker therefore never works (fields default to string).
close_reason: dropdown-select now item-scoped (item-signal-name + field-post/lock/unlock URLs + conditional id/name) and item-field option sources resolved via render/all-field-opts on both the page-render and collection-rerender paths. Verified by unit test + storyboard (q1 built as type=number).

title: SSE reconnect & session resilience
description:
Harden the Datastar SSE transport: client reconnect (Datastar has retry-duration), server-side recovery if a connection drops mid-edit, presence/lock cleanup robustness on abrupt disconnect, and a keep-alive so client disconnects are detected promptly. Also evaluate switching the SSE adapter to http-kit (true async, no thread-per-connection) if connection counts grow — currently ring-jetty9 sync, one thread per open SSE (see web/server.clj).

## Context
Phase 6. Thread-per-SSE-connection trade-off documented in PLAN.md.
notes: Done: server heartbeat (:datastar/heartbeat, hub/ping-all!) detects dropped clients via failed write -> on-close -> release-user-locks! + presence update + frees the parked thread (verified live: kill -9 a locking client, heartbeat auto-releases the lock). Datastar handles client reconnect out of the box (retryInterval 1s / retryScaler 2 / retryMaxCount 10) + full initial-sync on reconnect (existing). http-kit migration evaluated and DEFERRED: the heartbeat makes thread-per-connection self-cleaning/bounded, so ring-jetty9 is fine for moderate scale; http-kit (true async) remains the path for high connection counts — left as a future option, not needed now.
close_reason: Closed

title: Refactor (phase 5): shared store.clj persistence helpers
description: Dedup the copy-pasted store init: store/sqlite-datasource (documents+forms SQLite setup), store/edn-file-store (atom-or-duratom init across documents default/users/access/audit), store/read-edn + write-edn (EDN column/file codec). No new backends (users/access/audit stay single-backend; a 1-impl protocol would be ceremony).
close_reason: store.clj helpers; 220 tests + storyboard green (all persistence paths)

title: Refactor: move table.clj inline DnD JS to served /vendor/table.js
description: table.clj inlines ~150 lines of DnD/scroll JS as 3 def-string blobs (row-dnd-js, column-dnd-js, horizontal-scroll-js) emitted via [:script (h/raw ...)]. Move the bodies to resources/public/vendor/table.js as window.svRowDnd/svColumnDnd/svHorizontalScroll, loaded once in form.html; table.clj emits tiny inline calls per fragment (Datastar re-runs them so re-rendered tables re-attach). Preserves exact execution semantics, declutters table.clj, makes the JS a cacheable asset.
close_reason: done; 220 tests + full storyboard green (0 console errors)

title: Refactor: dedup field bind/lock attrs across input widgets -> components.bind
description: The focus->lock / blur->unlock / disabled-while-locked-by-another datastar attr trio (plus the edit-event POST) is copied across 9 input widgets (text, input_field, input_time, input_password, date_picker, dropdown, checkbox, checkbox_enabled, typeahead). Extract components.bind/lock-attrs + edit-bind-attrs. Add render tests (was uncovered).
close_reason: done; 220 tests + full storyboard green (0 console errors)

title: Refactor (phase 1 dedups): load-edn-dir + read-signals helpers
description: Collapse 4 copies of edn-file?+load-dir (forms/options/partials/terminology) into yogthos.stepvine.edn-dir/load-edn-dir, and 7 inconsistent (try (json/read-value (d*/get-signals req)) ...) sites into web.request/read-signals + read-rev.
close_reason: 4 of 5 dedups done (load-edn-dir, read-signals, opt-value/label, append-meta!); field-bind-attrs deferred (no unit coverage, flaky storyboard). 217 tests green.

title: Storyboard scenario 14 flaky: CodeMirror loaded from CDN (waitForSelector .cm-editor timeout)
description: Scenario 14 (Admin live app editor) intermittently throws on waitForSelector('.cm-editor', 20s) because the admin editor loads CodeMirror from a CDN — an external-network dependency that occasionally exceeds the timeout in CI/local runs. Pre-existing, lower-frequency flake; previously masked by the (now-fixed) scenario-16 broadcast-ordering flake. Options: bundle CodeMirror locally (removes the network dependency), or make the storyboard tolerate a CDN miss gracefully (soft-skip the editor-content assertions instead of aborting the whole run). Not an app-logic bug.
close_reason: Resolved by vendoring all front-end assets locally (resources/public/vendor, served at /vendor): datastar, codemirror (esbuild bundle), highcharts, calendarjs, lemonade. No CDN at runtime. Storyboard scenario 14 stable across 3/3 runs.

title: Parity: Advanced date-picker options
description: Ibis :date-picker supports min/max, disabled days, display/value format, and today/week helpers. Stepvine uses a native HTML date input with no such options. Add configurable date constraints and formatting to the date-picker widget.
close_reason: Date-picker: relative min/max (:today + offset maps), :step, server-resolved quick-set :helpers, friendly :caption; demo + unit tests + storyboard scenario 26

title: Parity: Responsive grid layout + section navigation
description: Ibis lays fields out with :row/:columns (Bootstrap 12-col) and :column-width [label content], and renders a section-navigation sidebar for long forms. Stepvine has h-box/v-box/box but no per-field column widths or jump-to-section nav. Add responsive grid layout primitives and a section nav sidebar.
close_reason: Responsive :c/grid (cols + per-field :span) and :c/section-nav jump-to-section sidebar; demo form + unit tests + storyboard scenario 25

title: Parity: Rich HTML / Markdown report view (beyond PDF)
description: Ibis :reports render hiccup/HTML/markdown with value transforms (:upper/:lower/:format/:join via :refs) and embedded widgets/functions. Stepvine exports are templated JSON + a clj-pdf step. Add an HTML/Markdown report view with value transforms and embedded widgets, viewable in-browser (not only PDF).
close_reason: HTML/Markdown report view: :reports section, value transforms, markdown blocks, in-browser route + demo + tests

title: Parity: Richer declarative validation rules
description: Ibis has cross-field and typed validators (:before/:after another date field, :field-must-equal another field, :contains for multi-select, :digits-only) plus :validate-when. Stepvine validation specs are single-field (required/min/max/len/regex) -- cross-field checks require hand-written reactions. Add declarative cross-field/date-comparison/contains/digits-only validators.
close_reason: Richer validators: :digits-only, [:contains x], [:before/:after f] (cross-field date compare), [:field-must-equal f]. field-error-reaction threads referenced fields as extra reaction args so errors recompute on either field change. forms/event.edn demo. validation_extra_test + storyboard scenario 22 green. 178 tests pass.

title: Parity: Modal data-entry sub-form (temp fields -> collection)
description: Ibis :modal hosts a sub-form with temporary (:tmp?) fields that commit to a table on a trigger (e.g. add-by-MRN). Stepvine's :modal-panel only toggles visibility of existing markup. Add a modal data-entry pattern: scratch/temp fields edited in a modal and appended to a collection/table on a trigger action.
close_reason: Modal data-entry sub-form: :c/entry-modal scratch fields commit to a collection row via /coll/:coll/add-from (populated add-item-with!, temp reset, modal close); demo + unit tests + storyboard scenario 27

title: Parity: Conditional and templated workflow step values
description: Aviary computes step parameters from context via a template language (:weaver/cond, :weaver/if, :fn/=, processors like :ldap/email, :report/get-in) -- e.g. choosing an email recipient by environment/field. Stepvine action steps take static values. Add templated step values (resolve {:from path}/{:reaction id}/conditionals) and conditional step execution (run a step only when a guard holds).
close_reason: Conditional/templated step values: resolve-value {:cond [test val … :else val]} (tests are reaction/value-specs); step :when for conditional execution. ticket.edn demo (cond email recipient + when-gated escalation). workflow_test + storyboard scenario 23 green. 180 tests pass.

title: Parity: Role/state-specific view selection
description: Aviary shows role-specific document views (nurse/dietitian/psychiatrist/surgeon) and gates editability by workflow state. Stepvine supports multi-view forms but does not auto-select the view by the viewer's role or the current workflow state. Add view selection (and field editability) keyed on role + state.
close_reason: State-based view selection (:for-states auto-select by workflow state, role-permitted) + state-based field editability (:writable-in render read-only + 409 enforcement). render-doc-view + render-node + apply-field; forms/case.edn demo. Role selection via 5aa per-view gate. Storyboard scenario 24 green. 181 tests pass.

title: Parity: External-service workflow step (HTTP / FHIR POST)
description: Aviary actions call out to external systems (:canary web service, :aidbox-bundle FHIR POST, EPR upload). Stepvine workflow directives are all in-process (:notify/:snapshot/:pdf/:set-field/:set-meta). Add a generic HTTP integration step that POSTs a templated payload to an allowlisted host (SSRF guard, like :http option sources), with error handling and optional retry.
close_reason: External-service :http workflow step: pluggable http.clj transport (recording dev/test + JDK prod), run-step :http with templated body, effects/perform! :http with SSRF allowlist, admin Outbox shows webhooks, ticket.edn approve demo. effects_test (resolve + allow/block) + storyboard scenario 10 green. 181 tests pass.

title: Example form: cascading derived fields (order calculator)
description: Add forms/order.edn demonstrating cascading Domino events where one calculated field feeds the next: qty,price -> subtotal -> discount -> tax -> total. Each calc field is an event output AND a downstream event input, so a single edit ripples through the chain (the lazy event DAG re-fires dependents). Plus a unit test (cascading_events_test) and storyboard scenario 16.
close_reason: Added forms/order.edn (qty,price->subtotal->discount->tax->total cascade), cascading_events_test (149 tests pass), and storyboard scenario 16 (live SSE cascade). Verified one edit ripples through the whole chain.

title: Editor preview supports multi-page forms (first-page fallback + per-view picker)
description: The editor preview hard-coded the :default view, so a multi-page form (no :default) previewed blank. preview-html now resolves the view (requested -> :default -> first page), returns the view list + rendered view via X-Preview-Views/X-Preview-Current headers, and the editor shows a 'Preview page' picker (multi-view forms only) to switch the previewed view, kept in sync with the edited EDN.
close_reason: Done: preview resolves view (requested->default->first page), exposes view list via headers, editor has a per-view 'Preview page' picker for multi-view forms. Storyboard scenario 14 covers it. 148 tests + storyboard green.

title: Shared page chrome: navbar, breadcrumbs, footer
description: A shared web/layout (navbar with brand + breadcrumbs + signed-in user + admin link + sign-out, and a footer) wrapping every server-rendered page (landing, document editor, index lookup, admin, auth). Breadcrumbs navigate to/from documents.
close_reason: Shared web/layout navbar (brand+breadcrumbs+user+admin link+sign-out) + footer across landing/editor/index/admin/auth. Breadcrumbs navigate to/from documents. 143 tests + storyboard chrome assertions green.

title: Phase 11 (§15.13): pages, index lookups, production backends
description: Multi-document workflow pages; index resolver multimethod; query-DB store backends; PDF generation; OAuth2/OIDC. PLAN.md §15.13.
notes: Done: PDF (clj-pdf), index lookups + document-list pages, pluggable document store + embedded SQLite query backend (DocStore protocol; atom + SqlStore). Remaining: OAuth2/OIDC.
close_reason: All §15.13 done: PDF (clj-pdf), index lookups + document-list pages, pluggable document store + embedded SQLite query backend, OAuth2/OIDC login. Completes the §15 reference-guided roadmap (Phases 7-11). 141 tests + storyboard (12 scenarios) green.

title: re-com parity: layout (scroller, border, gap/line) + alert-list, info-button
description: Remaining layout primitives (scroller, border, gap, line) and feedback (alert-list, info-button). Structure-only + external CSS.
close_reason: scroller/border/gap/line + alert-list + info-button ported, tested, demoed.

title: re-com parity: overlays (popover, modal-panel)
description: Port popover (anchored) and modal-panel (backdrop + centered panel). Server-auth: visibility bound to a signal; structure-only + external CSS.
close_reason: modal-panel + popover ported; local-signal visibility, tested + demoed.

title: re-com parity: tree-select + selection-list
description: Port hierarchical tree-select and the flat multi-check selection-list. Bound to array signals; structure-only + external CSS.
close_reason: Ported in commit; array-bound, reactive over signals, persisted + tested.

title: re-com parity: multi-select (dual list-box)
description: Port re-com multi-select — two side-by-side lists with add/remove between them, bound to a collection/array signal. Structure-only + external CSS.
close_reason: Ported in commit; array-bound, reactive over signals, persisted + tested.

title: Table/collection UI follow-ups: filter, column customization, nested-collection rendering
description: Deferred from the table/nested work: (1) filter dropdown is underspecified (no field binding) — design + wire coll-filter; (2) column customization (add/move/remove/label) cells are stubs — implement column config state; (3) nested-collection RENDERING — a nested :c/collection in an item template needs item-context path generalization (item-signal-name/field-post-url for deep paths), nested :collections scoping in the collection widget, and deep-path field-edit routes. Engine already supports nested data.
close_reason: All 3 follow-ups done: (1) table column filter wired (view-state filter + auto-derived values); (2) column customization (reorder/hide/restore/relabel via view-state overlay + path-posting DnD); (3) nested-collection rendering + deep-path edit/add/remove via generic /citem endpoints. Demos (tasks/ledger/teams) + unit tests + storyboard scenarios.

title: random-uuid warning from domino.util (external, alpha dep)
description: domino.util in domino/core 0.4.0-alpha.3 defines random-uuid without (:refer-clojure :exclude [random-uuid]), emitting a one-time compile WARNING. It's an external namespace (domino.core requires it), so unavoidable in-repo. domino 0.4.0 stable fixes it but is a ground-up rewrite (only transact/initialize/trigger-effects public) — incompatible with the vendored editor (needs get-downstream/select/set-value/...). Resolving requires porting the editor layer to domino 0.4.0. Deferred.
close_reason: Resolved by the domino 0.4.0 port — domino.util in 0.4.0 excludes random-uuid.

title: Per-document authorization (ACLs) + re-enable CSRF
description: P1 delivered login-gating (any signed-in user can access/edit any document — intended for the collaborative demo) and records :created-by on documents. Follow-ups: per-document access control (owner/shared lists, who may view vs edit), and re-enable ring anti-forgery/CSRF (currently false in system.edn so plain POST login/forms work). Consider rate-limiting login.
notes: Done: per-doc ACLs (owner :created-by + :shared set; can-access?/accessible-by/share!; owner-only delete). wrap-doc-access middleware on all /doc routes (404 missing, 403 no-access). CSRF: HTML form POSTs (login/register/logout/create/share/delete) use ring anti-forgery tokens; datastar JSON endpoints protected by wrap-require-datastar (requires Datastar-Request header, which datastar sets automatically — no token plumbing). Landing lists accessible docs only, with share/delete UI for owners. Verified live: 403 without token/header, ACL enforced, share grants access, non-owner delete is a no-op.
close_reason: Closed

title: Per-item reactions in collections
description:
Collections currently broadcast per-item *event*-derived fields (e.g. full name). Support per-item *reactions* (domino subcontext reactions) in the signal map + broadcast. Procyon's session_manager/rx-in-ctx walks subcontext reactions (vector ids [coll idx rxn]); render/collections-data + session->signal-map only read id->opts field values today.

## Context
Phase 4b deferral. See render.clj collections-data / session->signal-map; procyon rx-in-ctx for the pattern.
notes: Done: collections-data reads per-item reaction values via vector ids [coll idx rxn] (from the collection :schema :reactions); they flow into the item value map -> collection-signal-map -> session->signal-map, so per-item reactions broadcast like any signal. value widget is item-aware for :rxn. Demoed on roster :initials. Verified live (initials=GH recomputes/broadcasts).
close_reason: Closed

title: Per-item field locking in collections
description:
Field locking (focus/blur via procyon.editor/locks) is wired for top-level fields only. Extend server-authoritative locking + peer-visible disable to collection item fields (vector ids [coll idx field]). render.clj input-field skips lock attrs when (:item ctx); session lock fns + the lock signal model need item-scoped support.

## Context
Phase 4b deferral. See render.clj input-field, session.clj lock-field!.
notes: Done: per-item field locking via procyon e/lock!/unlock! with vector id [coll idx field] (verified items are independent — parent only gets :locked-children annotation, not a full lock). New endpoints /doc/:id/coll/:coll/:idx/field/:fid/lock|unlock; session/lock-item-field!/unlock-item-field!/apply-item-field-as! (lock-aware, rejects non-owner). lock-signal-map extended to item fields; input-field renders focus/blur + data-attr:disabled uniformly (item urls/signals coll-scoped). Disconnect cleanup (release-user-locks!) already clears item locks. Verified live.
close_reason: Closed

title: Form revisions & document migrations
description:
When a form's model/events/effects change, existing document instances may need migrating (not just field changes — event/effect incompatibilities too). Add form versioning and a migration path/dashboard. Per procyon NOTES.md.

## Context
Phase 6.
notes: Done: form :version (int, default 1) + optional :migrations {from-version (fn [db] db')} (sci-evaled). Documents record :form-version on create. docs/ensure! migrates on load: if doc-version < form-version, apply migration transforms in order, re-init against current schema (derived fields recompute), persist migrated db + bump version (idempotent). migrations.clj + documents/save-migration!. Verified via migrations_test (rename field + recompute derived, persist, idempotent).
close_reason: Closed

title: Visual form builder (Datastar UI)
description:
Author form :data/:views in-app instead of editing forms/*.edn by hand. A Datastar UI to define model fields, events, reactions, views/markup, exports; save via forms/save-form!. v1 authors forms as EDN files (decided); this is the deferred builder.

## Context
Phase 6.
notes: Done: the builder is itself a Stepvine form-document (forms/builder.edn) — top fields (form-id/title) + a :fields collection ({fid,flabel,ftype}) — reusing the whole engine (collections, reactivity, dropdown options field-types.edn). builder/build-form reads the live builder doc and generates a real form EDN (model + default view); :c/build-button -> POST /doc/:id/build -> forms/save-form! (disk + store) -> immediately creatable. Verified live: built a 'contact' form, saved to disk, appears on landing.
close_reason: Closed

title: Content search for documents (SQLite JSON1/FTS5, portable to Postgres jsonb)
description:
Decision: do NOT use XTDB/datalevin — overkill for a single-process app already on SQLite. The SqlStore backend (documents.clj) already provides indexed listing + owner/status filtering behind the DocStore protocol; the only gap is CONTENT search over form field values (currently stored as pr-str EDN in the :db blob, opaque to SQL).

When content search is needed: store the doc (or a projection of searchable values) as JSON instead of pr-str EDN, then add json_extract / FTS5 queries to SqlStore.-query. JSON1 + FTS5 are built into the bundled xerial sqlite-jdbc 3.49 (no new dep). Same approach maps to Postgres jsonb + GIN / to_tsvector. DocStore protocol keeps every caller backend-agnostic; atom backend stays for tests.

NOTE: speculative until a search UI/use-case exists — nothing requests content search today (queues + ACLs only use list + owner/status filters, already covered).
close_reason: Auth-scoped document content search (search-accessible over accessible-by) wired into the UI: /search page + navbar box, under wrap-auth. Backend-agnostic in-Clojure scan; JSON1/FTS5 optimization deferred. Tests + storyboard scenario 30.

title: Parity: Terminology / value-set binding for coded fields
description: Ibis clinical forms bind coded fields to value sets / FHIR terminology. Stepvine option sources are static/options-store/http/client with no terminology semantics. Add value-set-backed coded fields (FHIR ValueSet expansion) as an option source kind. Backlog / domain-specific.
close_reason: Value-set-bound coded fields: :value-set source kind (inline FHIR expansion + named terminology store), folded into options store; demo + unit tests + storyboard scenario 28
